title: Pyrus estiensis
taxonomy: Pyrus estiensis is a species of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae). It is classified as a member of the genus Pyrus, which includes pear trees. The species is native to the Xinjiang Uyghur Autonomous Region of China. Further classification would place it within the order Rosales and the class Magnoliopsida. As a member of the Rosaceae family, Pyrus estiensis is closely related to other fruit-bearing trees such as apples and pears.

intro: Pyrus estiensis, also known as the Chinese pear tree, is a species of plant in the family Rosaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its ability to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Pyrus estiensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pyrus estiensis include teas, infusions, and capsules, which are used to support digestive health and alleviate various symptoms associated with gastrointestinal disorders.
